comment_text.sn22.44:1.2: The “substantial reality” is the aggregates themselves, mistakenly believed to be substantive and therefore a suitable candidate for a self. For discussion, see SN 22.105.

root_text.sn22.44:1.2: “Sakkāyasamudayagāminiñca vo, bhikkhave, paṭipadaṁ desessāmi, sakkāyanirodhagāminiñca paṭipadaṁ.
root_text.sn22.44:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano ariyānaṁ adassāvī ariyadhammassa akovido ariyadhamme avinīto, sappurisānaṁ adassāvī sappurisadhammassa akovido sappurisadhamme avinīto,
root_text.sn22.44:1.6: rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ; attani vā rūpaṁ, rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.44:1.10: viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ; attani vā viññāṇaṁ, viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.44:1.11: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘sakkāyasamudayagāminī paṭipadā, sakkāyasamudayagāminī paṭipadā’ti.
root_text.sn22.44:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako ariyānaṁ dassāvī ariyadhammassa kovido ariyadhamme suvinīto, sappurisānaṁ dassāvī sappurisadhammassa kovido sappurisadhamme suvinīto,
root_text.sn22.44:2.3: na rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ; na attani vā rūpaṁ, na rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.44:2.7: na viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, na viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ; na attani vā viññāṇaṁ, na viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.44:2.8: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘sakkāyanirodhagāminī paṭipadā, sakkāyanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti.

translation_text.sn22.44:1.2: “Mendicants, I will teach you the practice that leads to the origin of substantial reality and the practice that leads to the cessation of substantial reality.
translation_text.sn22.44:1.4: And what is the practice that leads to the origin of substantial reality?
translation_text.sn22.44:1.5: It’s when an unlearned ordinary person has not seen the noble ones, and is neither skilled nor trained in the teaching of the noble ones. They’ve not seen true persons, and are neither skilled nor trained in the teaching of the true persons.
translation_text.sn22.44:1.6: They regard form as self, self as having form, form in self, or self in form.
translation_text.sn22.44:1.10: They regard consciousness as self, self as having consciousness, consciousness in self, or self in consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.44:1.11: This is called the practice that leads to the origin of substantial reality.
translation_text.sn22.44:1.12: And that’s why it’s called a way of regarding things that leads to the origin of suffering.
translation_text.sn22.44:2.1: And what is the practice that leads to the cessation of substantial reality?
translation_text.sn22.44:2.2: It’s when a learned noble disciple has seen the noble ones, and is skilled and trained in the teaching of the noble ones. They’ve seen true persons, and are skilled and trained in the teaching of the true persons.
translation_text.sn22.44:2.3: They don’t regard form as self, self as having form, form in self, or self in form.
translation_text.sn22.44:2.4: They don’t regard feeling as self …
translation_text.sn22.44:2.5: They don’t regard perception as self …
translation_text.sn22.44:2.6: They don’t regard choices as self …
translation_text.sn22.44:2.7: They don’t regard consciousness as self, self as having consciousness, consciousness in self, or self in consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.44:2.8: This is called the practice that leads to the cessation of substantial reality.
translation_text.sn22.44:2.9: And that’s why it’s called a way of regarding things that leads to the cessation of suffering.”
